# 'gs repo park' enters exclusive mode: it records every linked worktree
# in a durable manifest and removes the worktree directories, leaving the
# whole graph in the primary checkout. 'gs repo restore' leaves exclusive
# mode, re-creating the worktrees at their branches' current tips.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# An anchor worktree with a feature branch checked out: dev -> feat1.
gs anchor create $WORK/wtB --name dev -b feat1
exists $WORK/wtB

# Park from the primary: the worktree directory is removed, but its
# branches survive in the primary.
cd $WORK/repo
gs repo park
stderr 'Parked'
! exists $WORK/wtB

# The whole graph is reachable from the primary while parked.
gs ls -a
stderr 'feat1'
stderr 'dev'

# Restore: the worktree comes back with its branch checked out.
gs repo restore
stderr 'Restored'
exists $WORK/wtB
cd $WORK/wtB
git branch --show-current
stdout 'feat1'

-- repo/dummy.txt --
dummy
